mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal a contraction in aggregate demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced corporate earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 falls > 1% YoY and INDPRO falls > 0.5% MoM
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of cases where both GDPC1 and INDPRO decline.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as higher discount rates weigh on equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong demand for residential construction, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through sector-specific momentum.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST rises.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signal a robust labor market, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved consumer spending prospects.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of cases where PAYEMS and UNRATE move in tandem.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates stronger supply-side capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the federal funds rate above 2% signals monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as borrowing costs increase and economic growth slows.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2%.
